summary: I'm Jamie—a curious, people-loving (97% extrovert!) Organization & Talent Development professional who thrives on solving problems through data analysis, developing people programs grounded in insights, and collaborating with stakeholders for sustainable impact . Expect me to always be seeking ways we can improve people experience—through human-centered, systematic approaches.

bullets.text: Upskill HR team in {data|data analytics} to audit 300+ sensitive leave cases and refute cost assumptions via {data|evidence-based analysis}
bullets.original: Upskill HR team in data analytics to audit 300+ leave cases, successfully refuting cost assumptions via evidence-based insights
bullets.reason: JD asks for 'data literacy advocate' and 'auditing data irregularities' — this bullet hits both directly

bullets.text: Guided HR leaders to quantify $362K in {data|absenteeism costs} and create executive-ready {data|data reports} driving policy redesigns and talent development
bullets.original: Empower HR leadership team to present executive-ready deliverables on $362K in costs, driving policy redesigns and process improvements
bullets.reason: JD emphasizes dashboards/visualizations + data-driven decision-making — reframed to lead with the data analysis action

bullets.text: Conduct needs assessments with 25+ global {cross|stakeholders}, surfacing {data|learning gap insights} to co-design 3 tailored programs
bullets.reason: JD's top priority is 'partner with stakeholders to understand reporting and analytics needs' — leads with that

bullets.text: Develop process documentation, digital resource libraries, and workflows for 70+ {cross|cross-functional} staff, cutting onboarding time by 75%
bullets.original: Develop process documentation and operational resources for 70+ staff, streamlining workflows and reducing onboarding time by 75%
bullets.reason: JD asks for 'develop and document new processes to accurately report on critical data' — guides/workflows framing is more concrete

bullets.text: Facilitate training webinars for 600+ participants, increasing {ex|program understanding} and driving 78% enrollment rate
bullets.original: Coordinate communications and training materials for 600+ participants, supporting program delivery and driving 75% enrollment rate
bullets.reason: JD calls for 'data literacy advocate — training on analytics tools via job-aids and training materials' — this bullet mirrors that directly

bullets.text: Manage end-to-end lifecycles for 20+ projects, utilizing {data|feedback analysis} to optimize operational strategies and vendor performance
bullets.original: Manage end-to-end lifecycles for 20+ cross-functional initiatives, tracking milestones and progress to drive seamless execution
bullets.reason: JD emphasizes continuous improvement via feedback analysis and metrics — this variant surfaces that directly

bullets.text: Analyzed 2,000+ employee {data|survey data}, surfacing {data|insights} and key engagement drivers to recommend C-suite actions
bullets.reason: Strongest data analytics bullet — leads with the actual analysis action; JD prioritizes workforce data analysis above all

bullets.text: Led manager effectiveness initiative, translating {data|data} into an executive-ready presentation to inform leadership development strategy
bullets.reason: Translating data into executive-ready reports = 'dashboards and visualizations that tell a clear story' per JD

bullets.text: Managed weekly {prog|SharePoint dashboards} for people metrics, Net Promoter Score, and {data|survey insights} to drive employee recognition
bullets.original: Coordinated 5 cross-functional projects focused on survey development, rewards & recognition, and L&D to improve employee experience
bullets.reason: JD preferred qual mentions 'survey design' — surfaces this experience more directly

bullets.text: Improved employee experience via 5 {cross|cross-functional} projects in {data|survey development}, performance review, and L&D
Assisted leadership in headcount forecasting; analyzed historical {data|workforce data} to project future headcounts via {data|graphs}
bullets.reason: JD explicitly requires 'ability to work with sensitive employee data, maintaining confidentiality' — leads with that signal

bullets.text: Streamlined onboarding across 3 global sites; interviewed {cross|stakeholders} to clarify responsibilities and map new team workflows
Initiated a DEI Inclusive Leadership workshop for 12 senior leaders, later expanded to 23 global locations
Supported 5 {cross|cross-functional} HR projects in talent development, succession, and {ex|Employee Relations}; upheld alignment and {ex|confidentiality}
bullets.original: Led change management coordination for an M&A project, aligning cross-functional stakeholders and documenting decisions and outcomes
bullets.reason: Combined ER cases + cross-functional project breadth (succession planning, L&D, survey dev) to surface confidentiality + program scope

bullets.text: Managed {cross|cross-functional} HR operations spanning recruitment, compensation analysis, and performance management globally
Coordinated end-to-end international talent acquisition, including job descriptions, postings, and global compensation benchmarking
Drove a 566% increase in applications in 2 months through personalized global outreach and talent engagement
Planned and executed recruiting events and C-suite panels at top 3 universities, engaging 230+ students and generating 80+ applicants

bullets: Relevant Coursework: Organizational Psychology, Research Methods, Applied Statistics, Group Dynamics & Leadership

education.degree: BA in Psychology and Education Studies (GPA: 4.00)

projects: Consulting Projects: Enhanced Elanco Animal Health employer branding and talent strategy via survey design and execution
Master's Capstone: Examined non-monetary factors shaping employer attractiveness for YouTube via mixed-methods research
Scholarships: Freeman Asian Scholarship (4-year full-tuition), Tuck Bridge Scholarship (full)
skills: Data & PM Tools: Data Analysis (SPSS, Excel, Google Sheets, Tableau) · Project Management (Asana, Airtable)
Productivity: Knowledge Management (SharePoint, Notion) · AI Tools (ChatGPT, Gemini) · Microsoft Office (Word, PowerPoint)
Certifications: HRCI Human Resource Associate Certificate (in progress)

jd.meta: HWI — Talent Management Specialist II · Salt Lake City, UT · $72,000–$108,000

sections.title: Role Overview
sections.content: The {data|People Analytics} team uncovers {data|data-driven insights}, understands the talent lifecycle, and enables transformational change. This role supports {prog|people data and analytics}, creates {prog|dashboards and visualizations} through storytelling, and builds {data|data literacy} across the organization.

items.text: Acts as a {data|data literacy} advocate — broadening internal knowledge of analytics tools through {data|job-aids and training materials}
items.reason: ODN bullet 1: upskilling HR team in data analytics

items.text: Assists in creating {prog|dashboards and visualizations}; supports {data|workforce data} analysis for {data|data-driven decision-making}
items.reason: ODN bullet 2: analyzing workforce data, executive dashboards on $362K costs

items.text: Partners with {cross|stakeholders} to understand {prog|reporting and analytics needs}, answers questions, and provides recommendations
items.reason: InGenius bullet 1 (reordered to #1): needs assessments with 25+ global stakeholders

items.text: Helps develop and document new {coord|processes} to accurately report on critical data
items.reason: InGenius bullet 2: guides and workflows for 70+ staff

items.text: Builds {data|data literacy} through communication and {data|training on analytics tools}
items.reason: InGenius bullet 3: training webinars for 600+ participants

items.text: Supports {coord|process improvement} projects by delivering {prog|metrics} to support business case development
items.reason: InGenius bullet 4: refining strategy via feedback analysis

items.text: Analyzed {data|employee survey data} to report insights and recommend actions — {data|data-driven} HR decisions
items.reason: NextGen bullet 1 (reordered to #1): analyzed 2,000+ employee survey data points

items.text: Translating {data|data} into executive-ready reports to inform leadership decisions — {prog|data storytelling}
items.reason: NextGen bullet 2 (reordered to #2): manager effectiveness initiative
NextGen bullet 3: survey development preferred qual match

items.text: Ability to work with sensitive employee data, maintaining {ex|confidentiality}
items.reason: Vestas bullet 1 (reordered to #1): confidentiality in succession planning

items.text: Assists in employee relations cases, ensuring compliance and {ex|confidentiality}
items.reason: Vestas bullet 4 (swapped): ER case handling with confidentiality

items.text: Bachelor’s in HR, Data Analytics, IO Psychology, or related ✅ (MS Applied Org Psych)
Basic understanding of data analytics and statistics ✅ (SPSS, survey analysis)
Work with sensitive employee data + confidentiality ✅
Workday Reporting ⚠️ (not claimed — gap; entry-level, trainable)
BI tools (Power BI, Tableau, Looker) ⚠️ (SPSS + Excel foundation; gap noted)

sections.title: ✅ H1B Status
sections.content: HWI has no H1B LCA filings on record. Sponsorship unconfirmed — verify before applying.
